prompt: The date arithmetic result is wrong. What is the bug?
choices: minusDays modifies the date in place; the variable must be declared var
LocalDate is immutable; minusDays() returns a new LocalDate and does not modify deadline; the result must be assigned: deadline = deadline.minusDays(7)
minusDays accepts only positive values; use plusDays(-7) instead
LocalDate.of must be called each time a new date is needed
explanation: All java.time types are immutable. Calling minusDays(7) returns a new LocalDate representing 7 days earlier but leaves deadline unchanged. Fix: deadline = deadline.minusDays(7); or LocalDate earlier = deadline.minusDays(7);
